description: Two-signal proxy for observable AI deployment activity. Signal 1: government procurement orders. Signal 2: corporate annual report disclosures of generative AI. Neither signal is a complete or symmetric measure of total adoption.

government_procurement.description: Government AI procurement orders — contracts awarded or modified within the rolling window.

US.note: Federal contract awards (types A-D) with AI keywords in award descriptions. Action-date filter limits to the rolling window; contract start dates in examples may predate the window. Federal only.

China.note: CCGP returned a response but the total count could not be parsed. Central-government only; sub-national procurement not captured.

company_filings.description: Public company annual filings mentioning "generative AI" in SEC EDGAR. US: 10-K domestic companies. China proxy: 20-F foreign private issuers (primarily Chinese ADRs).

US.note: Will be populated on next workflow run (v3 fetch script).

China_proxy.note: 20-F = all foreign private issuers on US exchanges (Chinese ADRs dominant, not exclusively China). Will be populated on next workflow run.

top_examples.agency: Department of Health and Human Services

examples_note: Examples sorted by award amount (largest first). 'Contract Start' = period_of_performance_start_date, which may predate the rolling window. Contracts appear because a procurement action (award or modification) fell within the window.

composite_us.note: Transparent proxy score for US AI adoption visibility. Two-component: government procurement (log-scaled) + EDGAR disclosure rate.

composite_china.note: No signals currently available — CCGP blocked, EDGAR data pending next workflow run.

methodology_note: Adoption is a proxy for observable deployment, not total adoption. Procurement captures institutional purchase/rollout intent. Public company filings capture self-reported generative AI use. Both signals are subject to reporting asymmetries: US data is substantially more automatable than China data. Compare directionally only.
coverage_note: Coverage is asymmetric by design of public reporting systems. US: full federal procurement API + all SEC-registered public companies. China: central procurement portal only (often inaccessible remotely) + US-exchange-listed Chinese ADRs only.
caveats: 1. CCGP null = inaccessible from runner, not zero procurement. 2. 20-F proxy includes non-Chinese foreign issuers. 3. 'Generative AI' disclosures include strategy mentions alongside confirmed deployments. 4. Contract start dates in examples may predate the rolling window. 5. Composite scores are transparent proxies, not validated adoption indices.
